Polycab India Ltd Q1FY27 

➤ Revenue ₹8,209.70 Cr (+39% YoY)

➤ EBITDA ₹1,136.20 Cr (+32% YoY)
✓ EBITDA Margin 13.8% vs 14.5% YoY & 13.1% QoQ

➤ PAT ₹796.70 Cr (+33% YoY)
 
➤ Wires & Cables (W&C)
✓ Revenue (+39% YoY)
✓ Domestic Revenue (+43% YoY)
✓ International Revenue (-13% YoY)

➤ FMEG
✓ Revenue (+71% YoY)
✓ EBIT Margin 8.0% vs 2.1% YoY

➤ EPC
✓ Revenue (-11% YoY)
✓ EBIT Margin 11.0% vs 7.7% YoY

➤ Achieved highest-ever first-quarter Revenue, EBITDA and PAT

➤ Wires outpaced cables during the quarter, supported by strong on-ground execution

➤ Wires & Cables business delivered 39% YoY growth, supported by healthy domestic demand, strong execution under Project Spring, and higher channel sales growth

➤ International business recorded a 13% YoY degrowth; however, diversified global footprint and a healthy order book underscore strong forward momentum

➤ FMEG business posted an outstanding 71% YoY growth, with broad-based momentum across product categories. Fans continued to be the key growth driver, recording more than 2x growth over last year

➤ FMEG EBIT Margin improved to 8.0%, driven by operating leverage and a richer product mix, in line with Project Spring's long-term margin target of 8–10% by FY30

➤ EPC revenues declined 11% YoY due to project execution timing, but the business maintained an 11.0% EBIT Margin and continued to benefit from a robust order book and execution pipeline

➤ Management expects sustained growth supported by government infrastructure investments, strong domestic demand, ongoing capacity expansion, innovation and distribution strengthening

➤ Company remains focused on reinforcing its market leadership and delivering sustainable, industry-leading long-term growth

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS: PUNE PLANT TO SCALE EV & BATTERY COMPONENT PRODUCTION ⚡🏭

• ✅ Shivalik Bimetal Controls has announced that the Consent to Operate (CTO) received for its Pune Phase-I facility is valid till June 30, 2032, enabling phased commercial operations 

• 🚗 The Pune facility will manufacture value-added components and assemblies for automotive electrification and battery applications, including bus-bar connectors and PCBA assemblies, catering to OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers 

• 📈 The company plans to scale commercial production in line with customer qualifications, programme schedules and commercial requirements, strengthening its presence in high-precision EV components 

• 🏭 Management said the facility provides a scalable manufacturing platform for Cell Connecting Systems (CCS) and supports deeper participation in electrification, current sensing and advanced industrial applications 

• 🌍 Shivalik currently serves 300+ global customers through manufacturing facilities in Solan and Pune, with the new plant expected to improve wallet share and long-term revenue visibility 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The CTO-backed operational rollout strengthens Shivalik's EV and battery component manufacturing capabilities and supports long-term growth in value-added products.)

ASTRAZENECA PHARMA INDIA: CDSCO APPROVES FASENRA FOR ADDITIONAL RARE DISEASE INDICATION 💉🇮🇳

• ✅ AstraZeneca Pharma India has received CDSCO approval to import, market and distribute Benralizumab 30 mg/ml (Fasenra®) in India for an additional indication 

• 🩺 Fasenra (Benralizumab) is now approved for the treatment of patients aged 12 years and above with Hypereosinophilic Syndrome (HES) without an identifiable non-haematologic secondary cause 

• 🇮🇳 The approval was granted by the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) under the Directorate General of Health Services, Government of India 

• 🚀 The approval enables AstraZeneca to market Fasenra in India for the new indication, subject to any other applicable statutory approvals 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The label expansion broadens Fasenra's addressable patient base in India, strengthening AstraZeneca Pharma India's specialty portfolio and future growth prospects.)

BCPL RAILWAY INFRASTRUCTURE: EMERGES L1 BIDDER FOR ₹4.51 CR EASTERN RAILWAY PROJECT 🚆🏗️

• 🏆 BCPL Railway Infrastructure has emerged as the lowest bidder (L1) for an Eastern Railway, Howrah Division project under Tender No. ELD-125-WC-OT-39A-25 

• 💰 The project value is ₹4.51 crore (including GST). The company has clarified that it is currently the L1 bidder, while the formal contract/Letter of Acceptance is yet to be received 

• ⚙️ The scope of work includes provision of anti-climbing devices, fixing anti-bird discs on insulators, installation of UV-protected HDPE mesh inside portal booms and G-jumpers to improve the reliability of 25 KV AC overhead electrification (OHE) in the Howrah Division 

• 🇮🇳 The order is from a domestic government entity and falls within the normal course of business of the company 

• 📄 The execution timeline will be finalized after receipt of the Letter of Acceptance (LoA) 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Emerging as the L1 bidder improves order inflow visibility, though the contract award remains subject to receipt of the formal Letter of Acceptance.)

reports.text:
ITC HOTELS: TO ACQUIRE WELCOMHOTEL AHMEDABAD OWNER FOR ₹155 CR 🏨📈

• 🏨 ITC Hotels has executed a Share Purchase and Share Subscription Agreement to acquire 100% equity stake in GHK Hospitality & Infrastructures Ltd., the owner of 130-key Welcomhotel Ahmedabad 

• 💰 The acquisition values GHK at an enterprise value of ₹155 crore on a cash-free, debt-free basis, subject to customary adjustments. The investment will be made through a combination of primary subscription and secondary share purchase 

• 📍 The acquisition will strengthen ITC Hotels' owned asset portfolio in Ahmedabad across market segments by acquiring an established hotel that is already operated by ITC Hotels under an Operating Services Agreement 

• 📅 The transaction is expected to be completed during Q2 FY27, with no regulatory approvals required 

• 📊 GHK Hospitality reported turnover of ₹35.16 crore in FY26, ₹31.23 crore in FY25 and ₹25.62 crore in FY24, reflecting steady growth in its hospitality business 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition expands ITC Hotels' owned asset base, strengthens its presence in Ahmedabad and is expected to be value accretive over the long term.)

MAXPOSURE: RAISES STAKE IN UK'S NEUTRAL DIGITAL TO 96.2% 🌍✈️

• 🤝 Maxposure has acquired an additional 3.8% equity stake in UK-based Neutral Digital Limited, increasing its total shareholding to 96.2% 

• 💰 The latest acquisition was completed for ₹76.52 lakh (USD 79,776.90) at USD 6.3 per share, with the consideration payable in cash 

• 🌍 Neutral Digital specializes in immersive digital platforms and experiences for the aviation industry, including CGI animations, aircraft cabin visualization tools (Cabinly), immersive marketing experiences and UX/UI design services 

• ✈️ The acquisition supports Maxposure's strategy to expand its presence in Western Europe and North America, leverage Neutral Digital's established client base and strengthen innovation capabilities 

• 📈 Maxposure plans to acquire the remaining 3.8% stake, taking its holding to 100%, by the close of the second financial year from the SPA completion date, subject to the terms of the definitive agreements 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The increased stake strengthens Maxposure's control over a strategic overseas aviation technology business and supports its global expansion and digital innovation strategy.)

JBM AUTO: INCORPORATES STEP-DOWN SUBSIDIARY FOR E-BUS BUSINESS 🚌⚡

• 🏢 JBM Auto's subsidiary/JV JBM Ecolife Mobility Pvt. Ltd. has incorporated Greenpath Mobility Ventures Pvt. Ltd. (GMVPL) as its 100% wholly owned subsidiary, making it a step-down subsidiary of JBM Auto 

• 📅 The new company was incorporated on July 15, 2026, and the Certificate of Incorporation was received from the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) on the same day 

• 🚌 GMVPL has been established to participate in government tenders related to the supply, operation and maintenance of electric buses, CNG buses and other public transport vehicles, while also undertaking related mobility services 

• 💰 GMVPL has an authorised capital of ₹15 lakh and a paid-up capital of ₹1 lakh, with JBM Ecolife Mobility holding 100% of the equity through cash subscription 

• 🚀 The company is yet to commence operations and will serve as a dedicated vehicle for expanding JBM Group's public mobility business 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The incorporation strengthens JBM Auto's electric mobility strategy and enhances its ability to pursue government e-bus opportunities through a dedicated subsidiary.)

• ✅ Jubilant Pharmova has received a rectification order from the Income Tax Department, under which the entire tax demand of ₹22.10 crore for AY 2020-21 has been deleted 

• 📄 The relief follows the company's rectification application filed against the tax demand raised by the Assessing Officer 

• ⚖️ The company clarified that its appeal before the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) on the merits of the case is being pursued separately and is yet to be adjudicated 

• 📅 The rectification order was received from the Income Tax Department on July 15, 2026, providing complete relief from the disputed demand 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The complete deletion of the ₹22.10 crore tax demand removes a financial overhang and strengthens Jubilant Pharmova's legal and financial position.)

GAUDIUM IVF: LAUNCHES AI-POWERED SIGNATURE LAB, BEGINS 19-CENTRE EXPANSION 🤖🏥

• 🚀 Gaudium IVF has launched its AI-powered Signature Lab at a new South Delhi centre, marking the first milestone in its planned 19-centre expansion outlined during its IPO 

• 🧬 The new centre integrates AI-assisted embryology, including SiD (Sperm Identification Device) and ERICA (Embryo Ranking Intelligent Classification Assistant), to enhance sperm assessment and embryo evaluation alongside expert embryologists 

• 🏥 The proprietary Gaudium Signature Lab combines advanced embryology, AI-powered technologies and internationally benchmarked quality standards, and will be replicated across all upcoming centres to ensure consistent clinical outcomes 

• 🌍 Built on 17 years of fertility expertise, the company highlighted its European Quality Award from ESQR (Spain) and experience in handling complex infertility and recurrent IVF failure cases 

• 📈 Management said the expansion aims to meet India's rising demand for fertility care by combining advanced reproductive medicine, AI-powered embryology and standardized clinical protocols across its growing network 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The launch strengthens Gaudium IVF's technology-led expansion strategy, enhances service differentiation and supports long-term growth through its planned multi-centre rollout.)

reports.text:
**Gravita India | Multi-Metal Recycling Expansion**

**Business Transformation**
- Diversifying beyond lead.
- Building copper platform.
- Expanding value-added portfolio.

**RMIL Acquisition**
- Acquired **~99%** stake in RMIL.
- Acquisition value **₹560 crore**.
- Copper products capacity **31,200 MTPA**.
- Additional capex **₹160 crore**.
- Mundra recycling capacity **29,400 MTPA**.

**Why Copper?**
- EVs need **3x** more copper.
- Demand from renewables.
- Growth in power transmission.
- Data centre demand rising.
- Infrastructure spending supportive.
- Secondary copper CAGR **~14%**.
- Recycled share **55%** by FY30.

**Business Quality**
- Supplies diversified industries.
- Serves defence sector.
- Around **25%** export revenue.
- Utilisation at **40%**.
- Target **65%** by FY28.

**Margin Opportunity**
- Energy use lower **80–85%**.
- Lower production costs.
- Reduced carbon emissions.
- Focus on value-added products.
- Better margins than commodities.

**Growth Outlook**
- Revenue CAGR **38%**.
- EBITDA CAGR **36%**.
- PAT CAGR **27%**.
- Estimates for **FY26–28E**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Gravita is evolving into India's next multi-metal recycling platform through its strategic entry into copper recycling, positioning itself to benefit from structural demand across EVs, renewables, power infrastructure and data centres while enhancing margins through value-added copper products.**

RASI ELECTRODES: BOARD APPROVES SALE OF 2.277 ACRES LAND FOR ₹3 CRORE 🏞️💰

• 🏢 The Board has approved the sale/disposal of 2.277 acres (227.7 cents) of unencumbered land owned by the company at Manjankaranai Village, Uthukottai Taluk, Tiruvallur District, Tamil Nadu 

• 💰 The land will be sold to an identified non-related party purchaser for a total consideration of ₹3 crore 

• ✅ The Board took note of the shareholders' approval obtained through Postal Ballot and E-Voting under Section 180(1)(a) of the Companies Act, 2013, along with the Audit Committee's recommendation 

• 📑 The transaction will be completed as per the Agreement for Sale to be executed between the parties, and the company will file the detailed disclosure under Regulation 30 of SEBI LODR separately

MEDI ASSIST: INTEGRATES UIDAI'S AADHAAR OFFLINE VERIFICATION FOR SECURE HEALTHCARE IDENTITY 🔐🏥

• 🔒 Medi Assist has integrated UIDAI's Aadhaar Offline Verification Framework into its health benefits platforms after becoming a registered Offline Verification Seeking Entity (OVSE) 

• 🏥 The consent-based digital verification enables members to access health benefits with reduced paperwork, while lowering the risk of identity misuse, impersonation and document-related fraud 

• ⚡ The solution strengthens identity verification for insurers, employers and hospitals, helping reduce manual documentation, speed up claims processing and improve trust across the healthcare ecosystem 

• 📱 Aadhaar Offline Verification is being rolled out for cashless hospital admissions, while digital reimbursement claim verification will soon be available through the MAven app 

• 🌐 The capability will also be made available to TPAs, insurers and pension funds using Medi Assist's technology platforms, expanding secure digital identity verification across the health insurance ecosystem 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The integration enhances Medi Assist's technology platform, improves fraud prevention and strengthens its digital healthcare ecosystem, supporting long-term growth and operational efficiency.)

AURUM PROPTECH: TO ACQUIRE HOUSING.COM IN ₹458 CR SHARE-SWAP DEAL 🏠🤝

• 🏡 Aurum PropTech's Board has approved the acquisition of 100% stake in Locon Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (Housing.com) from REA India Pte. Ltd. through a share-swap transaction valued at ₹458.06 crore, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals 

• 📈 As consideration, Aurum will issue 1.98 crore equity shares to REA on a preferential basis at ₹231.42 per share. Following the allotment, REA's stake in Aurum PropTech will increase from 5.54% to 24.90% 

• 🤖 The acquisition aims to integrate Housing.com's real estate marketplace with Aurum's PropTech ecosystem to build a unified AI- and data-driven platform connecting consumer demand, developer inventory, brokerage, rentals and property transactions 

• 📊 Housing.com owner Locon Solutions reported FY25 audited turnover of ₹687.46 crore and provides property listings, digital marketing, lead generation, home loan brokerage and advertising solutions for the real estate sector 

• 📝 The Board also approved the issue of 51 lakh convertible warrants to promoter Aurum RealEstate Developers Ltd. at ₹231.42 per warrant, amendments to the Articles of Association for tag-along rights, and scheduled an EGM on August 14, 2026 to seek shareholder approval 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition of Housing.com is a transformative move that significantly expands Aurum PropTech's digital real estate ecosystem, strengthens its AI capabilities and enhances long-term growth prospects.)

MOBAVENUE AI TECH: OPENS SINGAPORE HUB TO EXPAND AI-POWERED ADTECH ACROSS ASEAN 🌏🤖

• 🇸🇬 Mobavenue AI Tech has commenced operations in Singapore, establishing the city-state as its regional hub for Southeast Asia as part of its international expansion strategy 

• 🚀 Operations will be led through MAITL Asia Pte. Ltd., enabling the company to scale its AI-powered programmatic media, audience intelligence, omnichannel customer engagement and consumer growth solutions across ASEAN 

• 📈 The company will adopt an asset-light, platform-led model, combining its proprietary AI technology with local market expertise to deliver scalable, outcome-driven advertising solutions 

• 🌍 Mobavenue said the Singapore hub strengthens its ability to serve clients across ASEAN and supports its vision of building a globally integrated AI-powered AdTech ecosystem 

• 🤝 The company currently serves 150+ brands across sectors including e-commerce, BFSI, fintech, travel, OTT, gaming, healthcare and retail, with operations spanning ASEAN, LATAM, the UK and the USA 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The Singapore expansion strengthens Mobavenue's international presence, enhances access to the fast-growing ASEAN digital advertising market and supports long-term growth.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
ACME SOLAR: RAISES ₹2,646.64 CR PROJECT FUNDING FROM REC FOR 450 MW FDRE PROJECT ☀️💰

• 💵 ACME Solar has secured ₹2,646.64 crore in long-term project funding from REC Ltd for its 450 MW / 1,800 MWh Firm & Dispatchable Renewable Energy (FDRE) Project 

• 🏦 REC Ltd will act as the sole lender for the project with a 20-year financing tenure 

• 📜 The project has a 25-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with SJVN Ltd at a tariff of ₹6.74 per unit 

• 🔋 The FDRE project combines solar power and Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) technologies to deliver reliable and dispatchable renewable energy 

• ⚡ ACME Solar currently has a diversified portfolio of 8,070 MW, including 2,990 MW operational capacity, 5,080 MW under construction, and ~18 GWh of BESS under development 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The large project financing strengthens execution visibility, supports capacity expansion and reinforces ACME Solar's growth in the FDRE segment.)

For nearly 2 years, one of the biggest drags on India's economy wasn't weak consumption or lower government spending.
It was industrial credit growth.
Businesses simply weren't borrowing enough to build new factories, buy machinery or expand capacity & this now appears to be changing.
The latest data shows industrial credit growth has started picking up again after a prolonged slowdown. It may not make the front page every day, but for long-term investors, this is one of the more encouraging signs in the economy today.

Why does this matter?

When businesses borrow money, it is usually to invest in productive assets. They build factories, purchase machines, expand production lines and increase working capital to execute larger orders.
In simple words, today's credit often becomes tomorrow's production. This creates a positive chain reaction across the economy.

Higher industrial borrowing leads to more capital expenditure & it creates demand for machinery, transformers, cables, bearings, automation equipment, construction machinery, logistics and engineering services.

As businesses expand, more hiring gets done. Higher employment supports higher incomes, which eventually leads to stronger consumption. That is how an investment cycle gradually gathers momentum.

The timing is encouraging because India is already benefiting from several long-term trends.

• Manufacturing is gaining momentum under the China+1 opportunity.
• Government infrastructure spending remains strong.
• Power transmission and distribution investments continue to rise.
• Electronics manufacturing is expanding rapidly.
• Private sector capital expenditure is slowly making a comeback.

A recovery in industrial credit can provide another layer of support to all these themes but we all know that rising credit growth alone is not enough.

We also need to understand that the quality of lending matters just as much. If businesses are borrowing to create productive assets that generate future cash flows, the long-term impact on the economy is positive. Reckless lending, on the other hand, eventually creates bad loans.

Fortunately, India's banking system is far healthier today than it was a decade ago. Balance sheets are stronger, NPAs have fallen sharply and most quality banks have maintained disciplined lending standards.
For investors, industrial credit is worth watching because it reflects business confidence. Companies don't borrow billions of rupees unless they believe demand will remain healthy over the next few years.

If this trend continues, it could support earnings growth across sectors such as capital goods, industrials, electrical equipment, power infrastructure, engineering and quality financials.

Sometimes, the most important changes in the economy happen very very gradually & a sustained recovery in industrial credit could be one of them.

Unihealth Hospitals continues to execute on its East Africa strategy - Commissions its Eye Care & Ophthalmology Department at UMC Victoria Hospital, Uganda

UMC Victoria Hospital, Kampala has commissioned its dedicated Eye Care & Ophthalmology Department, with the first cataract surgeries completed successfully. This follows shortly after the Board approved increasing UniHealth's stake in Victoria Hospital Limited from 50.00% to 99.81% through a strategic share-swap transaction.

✔️ Adds a new high-value specialty with significant long-term growth potential.

✔️ Management plans to expand into Phaco Surgery, Retinal Surgery, Glaucoma Management, Paediatric Ophthalmology and advanced diagnostics, positioning the hospital as a regional eye-care centre.

✔️ As UniHealth moves towards near-complete ownership of Victoria Hospital, future earnings from such specialty expansions are expected to accrue substantially to UniHealth shareholders.

✔️ The development reflects management's strategy of pairing ownership consolidation with continuous investment in higher-margin clinical services.

East Africa already contributes the majority of UniHealth's consolidated revenues, making developments at Victoria Hospital particularly relevant from a long-term shareholder perspective.

CSM TECHNOLOGIES: WINS WORLD BANK-FUNDED DIGITAL PROCUREMENT PROJECT IN MALAWI 🌍💻

• 🌍 CSM Technologies has secured a World Bank-funded international assignment from the Public Private Partnership Commission (PPPC) of Malawi to develop an Electronic Auctioning Platform (EAP) under the Digital Malawi Acceleration Project (DMAP)

• 💰 The project, funded by the World Bank's International Development Association (IDA), is valued at ₹3.21 crore (USD 0.32 million / ₹32.08 million)

• ⚙️ CSM will design, develop and commission a secure digital procurement platform featuring online bid submission, real-time evaluation, bidder anonymity and automated publication of results

• 🏛️ The platform aims to improve transparency and efficiency in public procurement, benefiting over 75 lakh broadband users, 2,000 schools and 500 government offices in Malawi

• 🤝 The company will execute the entire project lifecycle, including system development, integration, cybersecurity, testing, rollout, user training and post-deployment support

🟢 Impact: Positive (The World Bank-backed international contract strengthens CSM Technologies' global GovTech presence, enhances execution credentials and supports future overseas business opportunities.)

AMARA RAJA: COMMISSIONS CUSTOMER QUALIFICATION PLANT FOR LI-ION CELLS 🔋🏭

• 🏭 Amara Raja has commissioned its Customer Qualification Plant (CQP) at its Giga Corridor in Telangana, marking a key milestone towards commercial-scale lithium-ion cell manufacturing

• 💰 The facility has been built with an investment of ~₹500 crore and forms part of the company's broader ₹9,500 crore, 16 GWh Gigafactory programme

• 🔋 The CQP will manufacture cylindrical and prismatic lithium-ion cells across multiple chemistries for customer qualification and OEM validation before mass production

• 🚀 The plant has an initial capacity of 60 MWh, with commercial-scale 2 GWh cell production targeted to commence during CY2027. Cell supplies for customer validation are expected to begin in August 2026

• 🇮🇳 The facility was inaugurated by Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy, highlighting the state's push to strengthen India's advanced battery manufacturing ecosystem

🟢 Impact: Positive (The commissioning advances Amara Raja's EV battery manufacturing roadmap, strengthens its position in lithium-ion cells and supports India's battery self-reliance ambitions.)

MCLEOD RUSSEL: NCLT DISMISSES INSOLVENCY PLEA AFTER NARCL WITHDRAWS APPLICATION ⚖️🍃

• ⚖️ The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Kolkata has dismissed the insolvency application against McLeod Russel India after NARCL withdrew its petition

• 🤝 NARCL withdrew the application following the Sanction Letter dated April 2, 2026 and subsequent modifications issued for the company's debt restructuring

• 🏦 The insolvency petition related to an alleged default of approximately ₹444.55 crore, originally filed by HDFC Bank and later assigned to NARCL

• 📄 The NCLT has dismissed the matter as withdrawn, while granting liberty to revive the petition if the debt restructuring process fails

🟢 Impact: Positive (Withdrawal of the insolvency case provides relief to the company and supports the ongoing debt restructuring process, though the petition may be revived if the restructuring does not succeed.)

• ✅ CRISIL has also reaffirmed the company's existing ratings of CRISIL AA / CRISIL AA-/Stable / CRISIL A1+ on its bank facilities and debt instruments

ADANI POWER: SIGNS 25-YEAR PPA FOR 1,600 MW POWER SUPPLY ⚡📜

• 🤝 Adani Power has signed a 25-year Power Supply Agreement (PSA) with Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company (MSEDCL) for the long-term supply of 1,600 MW of thermal power

• 🏭 The power will be supplied from a 2×800 MW Ultra-Supercritical thermal power plant to be developed under the Design, Build, Finance, Own & Operate (DBFOO) model

• ⏳ The agreement secures long-term power offtake for 25 years, providing stable revenue visibility for the project

• 🪨 The company said the project has been allocated coal linkage under the Government of India's SHAKTI Policy

🟢 Impact: Positive (The long-term PPA strengthens Adani Power's order book, ensures revenue visibility and supports capacity expansion backed by assured fuel linkage.)

reports.text:
SEMICON INDIA 2.0: CABINET APPROVES ₹1.28 LAKH CR SEMICONDUCTOR MISSION 🇮🇳🔬

• 💰 The Union Cabinet has approved Semicon India 2.0 with a total outlay of ₹1.27 lakh crore to strengthen India's semiconductor ecosystem

• 🏭 The mission focuses on chip fabs, advanced packaging, semiconductor materials, chemicals & gases, equipment, R&D and talent development

• 🧩 The programme is built on six pillars: Chip Design, Machines & Materials, New Fabs, ATMP/OSAT Expansion, R&D and Talent Development, with incentives ranging from 30% to 75% depending on the segment

• 📱 The Cabinet has also approved a ₹62,500 crore Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme (MPMS) to further boost domestic electronics manufacturing

• 📈 The government targets ~₹4 lakh crore of investments, ~₹2 lakh crore of semiconductor production, and ~₹1 lakh crore of exports under Semicon India 2.0

🟢 Impact: Positive (The programme is a major boost for India's semiconductor and electronics manufacturing ecosystem, benefiting chip design, EMS, packaging, specialty chemicals, gases, materials and equipment companies.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
FERTILIZERS; CABINET APPROVES NATIONAL INVESTMENT POLICY FOR UREA-2026 (NIPU-2026) 🌱🧪

• 🇮🇳 The Union Cabinet has approved the National Investment Policy for Urea-2026 (NIPU-2026) to promote new gas-based urea manufacturing plants and strengthen India's fertilizer self-reliance

• 🏭 The policy introduces greater transparency by separating fixed and variable costs for new urea projects

• 📊 A Return on Equity (RoE) band of 12%–16% has been introduced for new investments under the policy

• 💱 The policy mitigates foreign exchange risk by allowing the conversion of fixed costs into INR after four years based on prevailing exchange rates

• 💰 The government estimates savings of over ₹250 crore per plant established under NIPU-2026 compared with the earlier NIP-2012

🟢 Impact: Positive (The policy encourages fresh investment in domestic urea capacity, supporting fertilizer self-sufficiency and benefiting companies involved in gas-based fertilizer manufacturing and related infrastructure.)

TVS HOLDINGS: HOME CREDIT INDIA TO ACQUIRE VARTHANA FINANCE FOR ₹967 CR 🏦🤝

• 💼 TVS Holdings' subsidiary Home Credit India Finance has signed a Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) to acquire 100% stake in Varthana Finance Pvt. Ltd. for ₹967 crore (subject to adjustments) 

• 🎓 Varthana is a specialised education finance NBFC, providing loans and financial support to schools, colleges, coaching institutes and vocational training centres across India 

• 📈 The acquisition will strengthen TVS Group's financial services platform by expanding into the secured and longer-tenure lending segment and enhancing its education finance franchise 

• 🏛️ The transaction is subject to RBI approval and other statutory approvals and is expected to be completed within 9 months from execution of the SPA 

• 📊 Varthana reported FY26 turnover of ₹398.31 crore, PAT of ₹18.65 crore and net worth of ₹574.23 crore 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition expands TVS Holdings' lending portfolio into the fast-growing education finance segment, strengthens its financial services platform and offers long-term growth opportunities.)

MIRZA INTERNATIONAL: ICRA DOWNGRADES CREDIT RATINGS 📉🏦

• 📉 ICRA has downgraded Mirza International's long-term rating to [ICRA]BBB+ (Stable) from the earlier rating

• 💳 The short-term fund-based and non-fund-based facilities have been downgraded to [ICRA]A2

• 💰 The rating action covers bank facilities aggregating ₹205 crore, including fund-based, non-fund-based and unallocated limits

• 📅 ICRA said the ratings will remain under surveillance and are subject to periodic review based on the company's financial and business performance

🔴 Impact: Negative (The rating downgrade indicates higher credit risk perception, which could impact borrowing costs and lender sentiment.)

Active Clothing Co. Limited Opens New Corporate Office in Gurugram to Strengthen International Marketing & Retail Operations

Active Clothing Co. Limited has inaugurated its new Corporate Office in Gurugram (NCR), focused on Company's new International Marketing & Retail Operations. The new office marks an important milestone in the Company's growth journey and is expected to strengthen both its global business development and expanding retail operations.

With exports to 30+ countries and long-standing relationships with leading global fashion brands, the Gurugram office is strategically positioned in the Delhi-NCR region to enhance international customer engagement, marketing initiatives and business development, while also supporting the Company's next phase of growth.

The new office will also play a key role in scaling NUEMO, the Company's recently launched multi-brand retail vertical. Following the commencement of store rollouts across Punjab in May 2026, the Company plans to expand across North India before scaling pan-India, making the new office a key enabler of its long-term retail strategy.

The Company believes the Gurugram office will strengthen its domestic and international presence while creating a robust platform for sustained long-term growth.

WEWORK INDIA: LAUNCHES MEMBER SERVICES PLATFORM FOR BUSINESSES 🚀💼

• 🏢 WeWork India has launched Member Services, a first-of-its-kind business services platform offering enterprise-grade solutions exclusively to its members through a curated network of trusted partners 

• 🛠️ The platform initially covers Admin & IT and HR services, with plans to expand into Finance & Legal, Sustainability & Governance, and Marketing over the coming quarters 

• 🤝 Members will gain access to vetted partners across services such as employee transportation, AI-powered hiring, executive search, employee wellbeing, corporate merchandise and IT solutions, helping simplify business operations 

• 📈 The company said the initiative aims to transform every WeWork India membership into a one-stop business support platform, creating additional value beyond flexible office space 

• 🌍 WeWork India currently operates 76 centres across 8 cities, serving over 1.1 lakh members across enterprises, GCCs and startups 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The new platform strengthens WeWork India's service ecosystem, enhances member retention and creates opportunities for higher-value offerings beyond workspace rentals.)

VINATI ORGANICS: INVESTS ₹17.11 CR IN WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARY 💰🧪

• 💸 Vinati Organics has invested ₹17.11 crore in its wholly owned subsidiary Veeral Organics Pvt. Ltd. by subscribing to 1.71 crore equity shares through a rights issue 

• 🏭 The investment will support Veeral Organics, which is engaged in the manufacturing of organic fine specialty chemicals, in line with Vinati Organics' core business 

• 📊 The investment will not change Vinati Organics' shareholding, and Veeral Organics will continue to remain a 100% wholly owned subsidiary 

• 📈 Veeral Organics reported FY26 turnover of ₹21.94 crore, up from ₹10.55 crore in FY25, reflecting business scale-up 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The capital infusion strengthens the subsidiary's growth plans, supports capacity expansion in specialty chemicals and reinforces Vinati Organics' long-term business strategy.)

MUKAND: SIGNS TERM SHEET TO SELL KALWA LAND FOR ~₹506 CR 🏗️💰

• 📝 Mukand has executed a Term Sheet with AGP DC Infra Pvt. Ltd. for the sale of around 9.2 acres of land in Kalwa, Thane, along with specified non-exclusive right-of-way arrangements 

• 💰 The transaction is valued at ₹55 crore per acre, with an aggregate consideration of approximately ₹506 crore, and the company has already received an advance of ₹10 crore 

• 🏭 The company stated that the land sale is not expected to impact its operations and is aimed at unlocking value from surplus land assets 

• 📋 The transaction remains subject to regulatory approvals, NOCs, land demarcation and due diligence, with the final consideration subject to revision based on actual land measurement 

• 🤝 The buyer is not a related party, and none of Mukand's promoters, directors or KMPs have any interest in the transaction 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The monetisation of non-core land assets is expected to unlock significant value, strengthen liquidity and improve capital allocation without affecting Mukand's core operations.)

EMS: RECEIVES ₹102.85 CR LOA FROM UP JAL NIGAM 💧📜

• 🏗️ EMS has received the Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from UP Jal Nigam (Urban), Varanasi for a sewer infrastructure project after being declared the L1 bidder earlier 

• 💰 The project has an order value of ₹102.85 crore (excluding GST) 

• 🚰 The scope includes laying of sewer networks and providing sewer house connections in 4 wards (Shivpurwa, Tulasipur, Birdopur and Kajipura) out of 18 problematic wards of Nagar Nigam, Varanasi, on a turnkey basis 

• ⏳ The project is scheduled to be executed within 24 months 

• 🤝 The contract has been awarded by a domestic government entity, and the company confirmed it is not a related-party transaction 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The LOA strengthens EMS' order book, improves revenue visibility and reinforces its execution pipeline in the water and sewer infrastructure segment.)

BOSTON COMMERCE: BOARD APPROVES CAPITAL REDUCTION TO WRITE OFF LOSSES 📉📋

• 🏛️ The Board has approved a Scheme of Capital Reduction to write off accumulated losses, subject to shareholder approval at the EGM on August 5, 2026 and other regulatory approvals 

• 📉 The company proposes to cancel 66.52 lakh equity shares on a pro-rata basis, reducing its paid-up share capital from ₹7.00 crore (70.02 lakh shares) to ₹35.01 lakh (3.50 lakh shares) 

• 💰 The capital reduction will enable the company to set off accumulated losses of ₹6.65 crore, improving its balance sheet and net worth 

• ⚖️ The company clarified that all shareholders will be treated equally, with no change in percentage shareholding and no benefit to the promoter group from the restructuring 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The capital reduction is a balance-sheet cleanup exercise that eliminates accumulated losses, improves capital efficiency and strengthens the company's financial presentation without diluting shareholder ownership.)

ATHER ENERGY: BOARD APPROVES ₹1,200 CR PREFERENTIAL FUNDRAISE ⚡💰

• 💸 Ather Energy's Board has approved raising up to ₹1,200 crore through a preferential issue of equity shares and convertible warrants, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals 

• 🇮🇳 The India-Japan Fund (NIIF-managed) will invest ₹200 crore through the allotment of 16.26 lakh equity shares at ₹1,230 per share 

• 🏍️ Hero MotoCorp will invest ₹960 crore by subscribing to 76.19 lakh convertible warrants at ₹1,260 per warrant, while promoters Tarun Mehta and Swapnil Jain will invest ₹20 crore each through 1.59 lakh warrants each

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹1,200 crore capital raise strengthens Ather Energy's balance sheet, supports future growth plans and reflects continued confidence from Hero MotoCorp and the India-Japan Fund.)

STERLING & WILSON RENEWABLE: AUSTRALIA UNIT INITIATES ARBITRATION AGAINST SHELL ⚖️☀️

• ⚖️ Sterling & Wilson Renewable Energy's step-down subsidiary Sterling and Wilson Solar Australia Pty Ltd (SWSAPL) has commenced arbitration proceedings against Shell New Energies Australia Pty Ltd under the London Court of International Arbitration (LCIA) Rules 

• 🌞 The dispute relates to the Engineering, Procurement & Construction (EPC) and Operations & Maintenance (O&M) contracts for the Gangarri Solar Farm in Queensland, Australia 

• 💰 SWSAPL has sought damages under two claim scenarios:

Scenario A: AUD 28.03 million + USD 1.64 million

Scenario B: AUD 20.60 million + USD 1.64 million along with interest and legal costs 

• 📈 The company stated that the expected financial implication is the recovery of these claims if the arbitration is decided in SWSAPL's favour 

🟢 Impact: Mildly Positive (The arbitration seeks recovery of significant contractual claims. Any successful award could strengthen cash flows, though the outcome remains subject to the arbitration process.)

Tata Elxsi: Guidance - Concall Update

The aspiration for FY27 remains a high single-digit growth rate, contingent on the healthcare business picking up. 

The Media and Communication vertical is expected to continue its growth over the next two to three quarters, driven by ramp-up of previous deals and confidence in closing new large consolidation deals.

The Transportation vertical is also projected to grow over the next two to three quarters, with strong performance anticipated from the US and APAC regions, offsetting some softness in Germany.

The Healthcare business is expected to see growth in the current financial year, despite Q1 FY27 being near flat due to delayed deal awards.

EBIT margins are expected to balance the impact of wage hikes with the reduction of one-off costs in Q2, followed by a sequential ramp-up towards Q4 as revenues pick up and wage hikes are fully absorbed.

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹495

**Key Highlights**
- Mylan exited entire **5.64%** stake.
- Block deal removes key overhang.
- Some investors may monetize holdings.
- Timing of further sales uncertain.
- Biosimilars business seen improving.
- Momentum expected from H2FY27.
- Scale-up of bUstekinumab.
- Scale-up of bAspart and bDenosumab.
- bAflibercept launch to support growth.
- Revenue CAGR of **15%** expected (FY26–FY28).
- EBITDA margin expected at **26–27%**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **DAM Capital maintains a Buy rating on Biocon, expecting the removal of the Mylan overhang and a stronger biosimilars pipeline to drive meaningful earnings acceleration from H2FY27 onward.**

BLOCK/BULK DEALS | Market Update

Major Transactions

- Biocon
  - Mylan Inc. sold ~9.44 crore shares at ₹400/share.
  - Large institutional buying by domestic MFs, insurers and global investors.
  - Key buyers included ICICI Prudential MF, HDFC MF, Kotak MF, SBI MF, Aditya Birla Sun Life MF, Canara Robeco MF, Franklin Templeton MF, Mirae Asset MF, WhiteOak MF, ICICI Prudential Life, Morgan Stanley Asia, Societe Generale and Vanguard.

- SG Mart
  - Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) bought 11.23 lakh shares at ₹650/share.
  - Public Sector Pension Investment Board (PSP Investments) bought 10 lakh shares.
  - HR Global Manufacturing sold 22 lakh shares.

- Just Dial
  - Irage Broking Services LLP net sold 2.87 lakh shares at ₹765.25/share.

Key Takeaway
- Biocon witnessed one of the largest institutional block deals, with Mylan significantly reducing its stake while leading domestic mutual funds and marquee global investors absorbed the shares, reflecting strong institutional interest.

**BERNSTEIN ON BIOCON**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Underperform
- **Target Price:** ₹326

**Key Highlights**
- Viatris block deal provides orderly exit.
- No material change to earnings outlook.
- Street optimism seen as excessive.
- High leverage remains a concern.
- Biologics investments yet to scale.
- Syngene growth needs acceleration.
- Policy changes increase competition.
- Biosimilar moat gradually weakening.
- $2 billion revenue target may take 4–5 years.
- Succession strategy remains science-led.
- Commercial execution needs improvement.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein remains Underperform on Biocon, believing that while the Viatris exit removes an overhang, structural challenges around leverage, competitive intensity and commercial execution continue to limit the company's earnings outlook.**

Recode Studios Acquires 51% Stake in Premium Beauty Brand Aflairza

- Aflairza operates in the premium beauty segment with strong consumer acceptance

- The brand has demonstrated superior value realization, reflecting its premium positioning

- Acquisition aligns with Recode's strategy to expand its presence in the high-growth Beauty & Personal Care category

- Company will also utilize its distribution network and operational expertise to scale the brand

- Founders will continue to partner with Recode in the next phase of expansion

It gives Recode a stronger presence in the premium beauty segment, where higher pricing power and better margins can create long-term value. Leveraging Recode's distribution, product development and execution capabilities could significantly accelerate Aflairza's scale over the coming years.

Recode will initially acquire a 33% stake in Phase I for an aggregate consideration of ₹8.32 Crore, with equal allocation between promoter share purchase & fresh capital into the company.

🇮🇳 Is India losing the global innovation race?

India's innovation lag isn't a govt failure; it's a corporate issue. The country spends just 0.65% of GDP on R&D, vastly below the 2.5-3% global norm for major economies.

Over 60% of R&D is state-funded. Meanwhile, top IT firms returned over ₹6tn to shareholders in the last decade instead of funding tech, choosing short-term margins over long-term R&D.

Tactical play: Pivot away from stagnating giants. Bet on deep-tech startups & niche mid-caps. The entry of foreign players & new competition will act as the catalyst for growth.

 🎯 Bottom Line: Avoid low-growth incumbents returning cash over innovating. Markets will heavily reward companies prioritizing long-term R&D with superior valuation multiples.

Prefer downstream OMCs over upstream SOEs.

#HEG | Demerger Special Situation

Sometimes the market does not value a company properly because too many different businesses sit inside one structure.

Stay till the end and let us know which of the demerged business you would want to be in!!

-------------------

HEG looks like one such case.

Today, HEG is a mix of:

• Graphite electrodes  
• Hydro power  
• Battery anode materials  
• Battery energy storage solutions  
• Solar + BESS renewable platform  

Now the company is splitting this into two focused listed entities.

1. HEG Limited

This will house the mature graphite electrode business.

FY26 revenue: ~₹2,568 Cr  
FY26 EBITDA: ~₹497 Cr  
EBITDA margin: ~19%

Capacity is being expanded from 1,00,000 TPA to 1,15,000 TPA by early 2028.

This remains the cash-generating industrial business.

2. HEG Greentech

This will house the future-facing clean energy platform.

It includes:

• 278 MW hydro assets  
• TACC battery anode materials  
• REPlus battery energy solutions  
• Solar + BESS IPP platform  

The interesting part?

The hydro assets alone are expected to generate ~₹300 Cr+ annual free cash flow.

So Greentech is not starting from zero.

3. The big optionality

TACC is setting up India’s first large-scale battery-grade synthetic graphite anode material facility.

Phase 1: 20,000 TPA  
Commercial start: April 2027  
Long-term target: 60,000 TPA by FY32

If this executes well, the market may stop looking at HEG only as a graphite electrode company.

4. Why this can create opportunity

Right now, the market is valuing one combined entity.

But post-demerger, investors may get two separate stories:

• A mature graphite electrode business  
• A hydro-backed Greentech platform  

That separation itself can unlock better valuation discovery.

5. Near-term trigger

The key event to watch is NCLT approval.

Management’s latest guidance is Q2 FY27.

Post that, the focus shifts to record date, listing timeline and separate valuation of both entities.

Simple takeaway:

HEG demerger is not just a split.

It is a chance for the market to revalue two very different businesses separately.

One is cash-generating.

The other is optionality-heavy.

That is what makes this special situation worth tracking.

Not a recommendation.

Aerospace Contract Manufacturing is structurally superior to Pharma CDMO.

Both are Contract Manufacturing businesses at their core, but the quality and duration of earnings are very different.

A Pharma CDMO molecule typically offers 10-15 years of visibility before patent expiry creates revenue reset risk.

An aerospace programme can run for 50-70 years, offers 4-5x longer revenue visibility, carries prohibitive switching costs and generates decades of aftermarket revenues.

Longer visibility. Lower reset risk. Stickier customers. More durable earnings.

That is why aerospace contract manufacturing deserves higher value.

Source: Nuvama Research, Aequs initiation report.

BROKERAGE REPORT DAILY

**KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES | Model Portfolio Changes**

**Large Cap Portfolio**
- Added SBI Life.
- Weight: 150 bps.
- Added TCS.
- Weight: 150 bps.
- Increased L&T weight.
- Now at 400 bps.
- Shifted ICICI Prudential Life.
- Moved to Midcap portfolio.
- Removed Mankind Pharma.
- Trimmed DLF.
- Weight reduced to 150 bps.
- Trimmed GMR Airports.
- Weight reduced to 160 bps.

**Midcap Portfolio**
- Added CRISIL.
- Added DCB Bank.
- Added ICICI Prudential Life.
- Removed Dixon Technologies.
- Removed Dr. Lal PathLabs.
- Removed Info Edge.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kotak increased exposure to select large-cap quality names while rebalancing positions after recent outperformance and refreshing its midcap portfolio with new additions.**

**NMDC | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Lump ore price cut by ₹250/t.
- Fines price cut by ₹150/t.
- Including taxes: ₹310/t & ₹190/t cut.
- Lump price: ₹6,745/t.
- Fines price: ₹5,815/t.
- Domestic fines at 50% discount.
- Five-year average discount: 56%.

**Industry View**
- Seaborne iron ore prices softened.
- Supply increased globally.
- Chinese demand remained weak.
- Iron ore at ~US$98/t.
- Prices down 7% since May-end.

**Key Takeaway**
- **NMDC's price cuts reflect weaker global iron ore prices, driven by higher seaborne supply and softer Chinese demand, while domestic prices continue to trade at a significant discount to import parity.**

**IRB INFRASTRUCTURE | CLSA View**

**Brokerage View**
- Outperform rating.
- Target Price: ₹34.5.
- Positive Q1FY27 toll trends.

**Key Highlights**
- Overall toll growth: 12%.
- June toll growth: 15% YoY.
- Mumbai-Pune Expressway: 9% growth.
- InvIT tolls grew 11%.
- India's No.1 toll platform.
- Re-leveraging equity provides upside.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains positive on IRB Infrastructure, driven by improving toll collections, stronger performance across key assets and continued value creation through equity re-leveraging.**

**AXIS BANK | CLSA View**

**Brokerage View**
- Outperform rating.
- Target Price: ₹1,550.
- AR analysis.

**Key Highlights**
- Focus on premium NTB customers.
- Deeper engagement with ETB customers.
- Deposit growth has improved.
- Retail term deposit share declined.
- Underwriting filters tightened.
- GNPL improved across segments.
- PSL compliance improving.
- Crop loan issue impacted costs.
- PSLC purchase cost up ~70% YoY.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains positive on Axis Bank, highlighting improving asset quality and deposit growth, while noting higher PSLC costs due to the crop loan issue.**

**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | MOSL View**

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,800.
- Revenue grew 15% YoY.
- LFL growth at 5.5%.

**Key Highlights**
- Metro stores remained flat.
- Non-metro stores grew well.
- Gross margin expanded 45 bps.
- Higher GM&A mix supported margins.
- Employee cost rose 32% YoY.
- Added 3 stores in Q1FY27.
- Store expansion remains key.
- DMart Ready exited 7 cities.
- Presence reduced to 11 cities.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL remains positive on Avenue Supermarts, supported by healthy profitability, continued store expansion and stronger non-metro performance despite slower LFL growth.**

**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | Jefferies View**

**Brokerage View**
- Hold rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,500.
- Q1 better than headline suggests.
- Q4 pre-buying impacted Q1.

**Key Highlights**
- Older metro stores flat YoY.
- Quick commerce remains a concern.
- Gross margin at multi-quarter high.
- Staff cost rose over 30%.
- EBITDA margin expansion limited.
- DMart Ready exited 7 cities.
- Now present in 11 cities.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains neutral on Avenue Supermarts, noting resilient underlying demand but highlighting quick commerce pressure, rising employee costs and a narrower DMart Ready footprint.**

**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | Citi View**

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating.
- Target Price: ₹3,400.
- Revenue/EPS below estimates.
- FY27–29 revenue estimates cut 4–6%.
- FY27–29 EPS estimates cut 5–7%.

**Key Highlights**
- SSG moderated to 5.5%.
- Older metro stores remained flat.
- Non-metro stores grew well.
- GM&A mix improved.
- QC competition impacted growth.
- DMart Ready exited 7 cities.
- Now present in 11 cities.
- Subsidiary revenue growth slowed.
- Losses increased 32% YoY.
- Profit lagged revenue growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bearish on Avenue Supermarts, citing sustained quick commerce pressure, slowing same-store growth and earnings downgrades despite healthy non-metro performance.**

**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | JPMorgan View**

**Brokerage View**
- Neutral rating.
- Target Price cut to ₹4,250.
- EBITDA margin stable.
- Gross margin expanded.
- Weak LFL growth a key concern.

**Key Highlights**
- LFL growth at 5.5%.
- Metro stores showed flat growth.
- Quick commerce impacted demand.
- Non-metro stores performed better.
- Focus on non-metro expansion.
- Added 3 stores in Q1.
- Total store count: 503.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan expects DMart's stock performance to remain subdued as weak like-for-like growth and quick commerce pressures offset stable margin performance.**

**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | Goldman Sachs View**

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,000.
- Q1FY27 revenue growth slowed.
- Large store additions.
- Higher FMCG inflation.

**Key Highlights**
- Metro growth remained flat.
- Q-commerce impacted demand.
- DMart Ready targets large cities.
- EBITDA margin remained flat.
- Higher operating costs offset gains.
- PBT grew 11.9% YoY.
- Higher depreciation & interest costs.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains cautious on Avenue Supermarts, citing slowing revenue growth, rising competitive pressure from quick commerce and higher operating costs despite continued store expansion.**

**LTIMINDTREE | MOSL View**

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,800.
- Revenue and margin beat.
- FY27 outlook positive.

**Key Highlights**
- Q2 recovery on track.
- Margins surprised positively.
- BFSI-led growth.
- Technology-led growth.
- FY27 growth above FY26 expected.
- Among fastest-growing IT peers.
- Productivity transition largely completed.
- Advantage over peers in FY27.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL remains positive on LTIMindtree, citing improving execution, strong margins and management's confidence of stronger FY27 growth driven by BFSI and Technology.**

**LTIMINDTREE | DAM Capital View**

**Brokerage View**
- Neutral rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,050.
- Q1 CC revenue up 0.3% QoQ.
- CC revenue up 6.4% YoY.
- EBIT margin at 15.5%.

**Key Highlights**
- Banking grew 3.2% QoQ.
- Technology grew 3.4% QoQ.
- Production declined 5.7% QoQ.
- North America led growth.
- Europe & APAC declined.
- Margin up 40 bps QoQ.
- Utilization improved 70 bps.
- Deal wins: USD 1.68 billion.
- Book-to-bill ratio: 1.4x.

**Key Takeaway**
- **DAM Capital remains Neutral on LTIMindtree, supported by healthy margins and deal wins, while mixed vertical performance and regional weakness keep the outlook balanced.**

**LTIMINDTREE | Citi View**

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating.
- Target Price: ₹3,450.
- Q1 revenue flattish QoQ.
- PAT supported by one-time gain.
- FY26–28 revenue CAGR: 5%.
- Trades at 19.5x FY27 earnings.

**Key Highlights**
- EBIT supported by lower SG&A.
- Subcontracting costs increased.
- TTM TCV up 7% YoY.
- Headcount growth moderated.
- Management confident on Q2.
- Momentum expected into H2.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains cautious on LTIMindtree despite positive management commentary, citing modest organic growth expectations and reliance on one-time factors in Q1 performance.**

**LTIMINDTREE | Jefferies View**

**Brokerage View**
- Underperform rating.
- Target Price: ₹3,360.
- Q1 broadly in line.
- EPS cut by 2–3%.
- FY27–29 revenue CAGR: 6%.
- FY27–29 EPS CAGR: 9%.

**Key Highlights**
- FY27 growth target seen optimistic.
- Productivity gains largely behind.
- Weaker growth outlook.
- Slow new client additions.
- Derating risk remains.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains cautious on LTIMindtree, citing an optimistic growth outlook, weaker client additions and limited earnings growth, leading to an Underperform rating.**

**L&T FINANCE | JPMorgan View**

**Brokerage View**
- JPM maintains Neutral.
- Target Price: ₹320.
- Q1FY27 PAT beats estimate by 4%.
- PPoP beats estimate by 4%.
- NII marginally below estimate.

**Key Highlights**
- Higher fee income offset lower NIM.
- Lower operating expenses.
- NIM declined 10 bps QoQ.
- Better-than-expected asset quality.
- Credit cost at 2.55%.
- Net slippages increased QoQ.
- RoA improved to 2.48%.
- AUM grew 27% YoY.
- Retail-led growth continued.

**Valuation**
- Stock up 23% in 1 month.
- Valuation at 2.3x FY28E PB.
- Further re-rating may be limited.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan acknowledges a strong Q1FY27 with improving profitability and asset quality but remains Neutral, citing rich valuations and the need to monitor asset quality trends amid monsoon-related risks.**

**L&T FINANCE | Nomura View**

**Brokerage View**
- Nomura maintains Buy.
- Target Price: ₹370.
- Q1FY27 profit beats estimate by 8%.
- FY27–FY29 EPS raised by 2–10%.

**Key Highlights**
- Encouraging asset-quality trends.
- 2W segment improving.
- AI engines supporting underwriting.
- Risks: Monsoon.
- Risks: Insurance distribution reforms.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains positive on L&T Finance, supported by better-than-expected Q1FY27 earnings, improving asset quality and higher earnings estimates despite near-term risks.**

**CARTRADE | UBS Initiates Coverage**

**Brokerage View**
- UBS initiates with Buy.
- Target Price: ₹4,000.
- Asset-light business model.
- Significant operating leverage.
- 15–20% earnings upside.
- FY27–FY29E consensus estimates.

**Growth & Margin Outlook**
- Revenue at only 3–4% of TAM.
- Significant top-line growth potential.
- OLX dominates used C2C platform.
- FY23 margin: 9%.
- FY26 margin: 33%.
- FY30E margin: 47%.
- Global peers: 50–60% margins.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS expects CarTrade's asset-light model, margin expansion, and large untapped market opportunity to drive strong earnings growth over the coming years.**

Goldman Sachs: India Strategy | Room To Rebound

India strategy turned more positive: Target Nifty 26,500 by Jun'27 with expectation of FII flows returning and domestic recovery strengthening #ym

*Key calls are* Value over Growth, Large Caps over Mid Caps, Banks/Utilities/Tourism/Defense as preferred themes, while Metals, Cement, IT/Pharma exporters and rural plays are less favored 

Sector changes: 

*Upgraded
> Utilities → Overweight 
> Industrials → Market Weight 

*Downgraded
> Staples → Market Weight (from a more positive stance) 
> Metals & Mining → Underweight 
> Cement → Underweight 

*Unchanged positive
> Banks → Overweight
> Energy Refiners → Overweight 
> TMT → Overweight 
> Defense → Overweight 

*Unchanged negative
> IT Exporters → Underweight 
> Pharma Exporters → Underweight
> OMCs/Downstream Oil → Underweight

Goldman Sachs -India Strategy: Room to rebound  
India’s outlook has improved in recent weeks, amid lower commodity prices, stabilized currency, resilient domestic growth, healthy earnings expectations in 2Q, and potential recovery in select domestic pockets. With ultra-light foreign positioning, we see ample room for flows to return. While renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East may fuel near-term volatility, we see room for NIFTY to recover towards our June 2027 target of 26,500, implying c.10% upside from current levels following a 9% drawdown in 1H

Goldman Sachs - India Strategy: Room to rebound
Structurally, we remain bullish on defense and energy security. Sectorally, we raise utilities to overweight and remain overweight on banks, energy refiners, TMT, and defense. We
stay underweight exporters, downstream oil and downgrade select materials to underweight

Goldman Sachs -India Strategy: Room to rebound
Identify 15 large-cap stocks that could benefit from key themes & catalysts in 2H
RIL, HDFC BK, Adani Ent, Adani Power, Kotak BK
NTPC, HAL, Eternal, Power Grid, Adani Green
Interglobe, HDFC Life Insurance, Indian Hotels, Mazagaon Dock &Makemytrip

**JM Financial on AIA Engineering (AIAE)
Maintain ADD rating 
Increase TP to ₹6,000 vs ₹4,915
Increase PE multiple to 35x vs 30x**

Key positives:
-Patented grate discharge ball mills are helping AIAE penetrate the forged grinding media market with high-chrome offerings.
-Next-Gen DE ball mills could drive EBITDA above JM estimates.
-AIAE offers better value than Tega.
Valuation: JM values AIAE and Tega's core business (ex-Molycop) at 35x Sep'28 P/E, but assigning Molycop an 18x Sep'28E P/E due to its low single-digit growth outlook through FY30E
